Problem:

{"description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US01
**Goal**: When a new user signs up, their workspace, actor identity, and default seed data (3 Areas + 5 Contexts) are automatically created before the user ever reaches the application.
**Acceptance**: Register a new account, immediately hit any authenticated endpoint, verify workspace + actor + areas + contexts all exist. GET /api/v1/areas returns 3 seeded areas. GET /api/v1/contexts returns 5 seeded contexts.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[Critical] requireWorkspace: empty actorId must return 503, not fall through** (workspace-35c)
Add explicit null guard: if (!actorId) return c.json({ error: 'Workspace not provisioned' }, 503); — falling through with empty string produces silent data corruption (audit events with empty actor_id, actions owned by nobody).

**[Critical] D1 batch FK ordering: audit events must be inserted last** (workspace-svs)
Order D1 batch statements: 1) workspace, 2) actor, 3) areas+contexts, 4) audit events. Audit events have FKs on workspace_id and actor_id which must exist first or the batch fails with FK constraint errors.

**[High] All D1 statements must use .bind() — no string interpolation** (workspace-40o)
WorkspaceProvisioningService must use parameterized statements (.bind(...params)) for all external values. Direct string interpolation in SQL is a SQL injection vulnerability.

**[High] Provisioning audit events: actor_id must be the newly created human actor ID** (workspace-85o)
Use the newly created human actor's ID for all provisioning audit events (workspace, areas, contexts). Do not leave actor_id empty or use a synthetic system actor.

**[High] WorkspaceProvisioningService must be a thin D1-batch adapter called by ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase** (workspace-v4b)
Do not put domain orchestration in WorkspaceProvisioningService. The auth hook calls ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase; the use case calls the service as a D1-batch transport. Business logic stays in the application layer."}

GET /api/v1/areas returns 3 seeded areas. GET /api/v1/contexts returns 5 seeded contexts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:05Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:05Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US03
**Goal**: A user can instantly capture a thought/task/commitment to their inbox without upfront categorisation. POST /api/v1/inbox creates inbox item. GET /api/v1/inbox lists all workspace inbox items.
**Acceptance**: POST with title → 201 + inbox item with status=inbox + audit event. POST with empty title → 400. GET → list of workspace inbox items.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[High] InboxItemRepository interface must accept optional status filter** (workspace-2bk)
Define listByWorkspaceId(workspaceId: string, status?: InboxItemStatus): Promise<InboxItem[]> in the domain interface. Without the optional param the concrete D1 class will not satisfy the interface and TypeScript will error.

**[Critical] GET /api/v1/inbox filters to status='inbox' only — document this explicitly** (workspace-7tc)
The plan silently filters to status='inbox' only, but the spec says 'list of workspace inbox items' with no filter. Explicitly state in the acceptance criteria that GET /api/v1/inbox returns only status='inbox' items (clarified items are not shown). Accepted decision: filter to inbox status only, matching the plan.

**[Medium] Validate Content-Type: application/json before calling req.json()** (workspace-89u)
Add Content-Type header validation (or a Hono middleware) before all req.json() calls in InboxApiHandlers to avoid parse errors and unexpected behavior with malformed requests."}

POST /api/v1/inbox creates inbox item. GET /api/v1/inbox lists all workspace inbox items.\n**Acceptance**: POST with title → 201 + inbox item with status=inbox + audit event. POST with empty title → 400. GET → list of workspace inbox items.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:13Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:13Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US04
**Goal**: A user can process an inbox item and turn it into a concrete, actionable task by assigning it an Area and Context. Atomic D1 batch: inbox item → clarified, new action → ready.
**Acceptance**: POST /api/v1/inbox/:id/clarify with areaId+contextId → inbox item status=clarified + new action status=ready + 2 audit events. Already-clarified item → 422. Invalid area → 422. Invalid context → 422.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[Critical] ClarifyInboxItemToAction: use optimistic lock via WHERE status='inbox'** (workspace-gib)
Implement atomic conditional UPDATE: UPDATE inbox_item SET status='clarified' WHERE id=? AND status='inbox'; check rows affected = 1 before proceeding with action creation. Without this, concurrent clarify requests cause a TOCTOU race producing double-clarification.

**[High] Clarify request: description is an optional override, not required** (workspace-g0u)
The action inherits the inbox item's description automatically. The POST /api/v1/inbox/:id/clarify request body may accept an optional description field to override the inherited value. Update FR-002 and openapi.yaml to make this explicit.

**[High] Define ClarifyInboxItemResultDto { inboxItem, action } in data-model.md** (workspace-40y)
Add ClarifyInboxItemResultDto to the DTOs section of data-model.md matching the { inboxItem, action } shape already in openapi.yaml. The response shape must not be invented ad hoc at implementation time.

**[High] Migration: add clarified_into_id FK with REFERENCES action(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT** (workspace-089)
The clarified_into_id column on inbox_item table must have REFERENCES action(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT to prevent orphaned clarification references. Include this constraint in the D1 migration for the inbox_item table."}
